title: MT-RNR1 / aminoglycoside antibacterials
summary: The rs267606618 C allele (also known as the 1095C allele) is assigned as a MT-RNR1 increased risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss allele by CPIC. Patients with the C allele may have an increased risk of experiencing aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss as compared to patients with the T allele. MT-RNR1 is a mitochondrial gene and is inherited as a haploid (i.e. there is only ever one allele present). Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss.
riskSummary: MT-RNR1 variant associated with toxicity for aminoglycoside antibacterials

MT-RNR1 is a mitochondrial gene and is inherited as a haploid (i.e. there is only ever one allele present). Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs397508442 CC genotype (do not have a copy of the CFTR S945L variant) and cystic fibrosis have an unknown response to ivacaftor treatment, as response may depend on the presence of other CFTR variants. title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs397508442 CT genotype (one copy of the CFTR S945L variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of S945L. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
